name: OpenAI Agents SDK
description: Production-ready multi-agent orchestration framework built around agents, handoffs, guardrails, and tracing. Open-source (MIT) successor to Swarm, released March 2025 with a major overhaul in April 2026 that added a model-native harness (filesystem tools, shell execution, apply-patch edits) and native sandboxed execution for long-horizon tasks.

evidence.source: OpenAI: New tools for building agents
evidence.value: Production-grade upgrade of Swarm with structured outputs, validated handoffs, and Responses API integration

task_completion_accuracy.methodology: Evaluation of agent task success across single- and multi-agent configurations

evidence.value: Function tools with automatic Pydantic schema validation plus hosted tools (web search, file search, code interpreter, computer use)

tool_use_reliability.methodology: Testing of function tools, hosted tools, and schema-validated argument handling

evidence.value: Agent loop with configurable max turns handles multi-step tasks; planning quality depends on the configured model

multi_step_planning.methodology: Multi-step task evaluation across the agent loop and handoff chains

evidence.value: Built-in session memory (SQLite, Redis, SQLAlchemy backends) automatically maintains conversation history across runs

memory_persistence.methodology: Review of session backends and cross-run conversation persistence

evidence.value: Typed exceptions, guardrail tripwires, and tool error handlers enable structured failure handling

error_recovery.methodology: Testing of exception handling, guardrail tripwires, and tool failure paths

evidence.value: Handoffs are a core primitive for delegating between specialized agents; agents-as-tools pattern supports orchestrator designs

agent_collaboration.methodology: Multi-agent coordination testing using handoffs and agents-as-tools patterns

evidence.value: No built-in sandbox for arbitrary custom function tools; hosted tools (code interpreter) run in OpenAI's sandboxed environments

evidence.source: OpenAI: The next evolution of the Agents SDK
evidence.value: April 2026 overhaul added native sandbox execution for harness work (files, shell, code edits), launching first in Python with TypeScript to follow

tool_sandboxing.methodology: Security architecture review of custom tool execution versus hosted tools
tool_sandboxing.notes: Score raised 65 to 75: April 2026 release added native sandboxed execution for harness/shell/file operations; custom function tools still execute unsandboxed

evidence.value: Per-agent tool restrictions and human-in-the-loop approval support; broader access control left to the developer

access_control.methodology: Assessment of tool scoping, approval flows, and developer-implemented controls

evidence.value: First-class input/output guardrails run in parallel with the agent and can trip to halt unsafe or off-policy runs

prompt_injection_defense.methodology: Guardrail configuration testing against adversarial and off-policy inputs

evidence.value: Typed local context objects are isolated per run and never sent to the LLM; deployment isolation is integrator-managed

data_isolation.methodology: Review of run context isolation and self-hosted deployment boundaries

evidence.value: MIT licensed, fully open source with active public development in Python and TypeScript
API data not used for training by default; session state stored on the integrator's own backend

data_retention.methodology: Review of OpenAI API retention terms and self-managed session storage

evidence.value: OpenAI offers DPA and SOC 2 for API usage; framework itself is self-hosted so compliance is achievable with configuration

gdpr_compliance.methodology: Compliance capabilities assessment for framework plus default model provider

evidence.value: Tracing uploads run data to OpenAI by default (disableable); prompts go to whichever LLM provider is configured

third_party_data_sharing.methodology: Data flow analysis of default tracing export and model provider routing

evidence.value: LiteLLM integration supports 100+ LLMs including local models via Ollama-compatible endpoints

local_deployment_option.methodology: Deployment options assessment including non-OpenAI and local model routing

evidence.value: Comprehensive docs with quickstarts, API reference, and examples for agents, handoffs, guardrails, sessions, and tracing

documentation_quality.methodology: Documentation completeness review across Python and TypeScript SDKs

evidence.value: Built-in tracing of every LLM call, tool call, handoff, and guardrail with OpenAI dashboard and OTel/third-party exporters

execution_traceability.methodology: Review of built-in trace spans, dashboard visualization, and OpenTelemetry export

evidence.value: Trace spans expose handoff reasons, tool arguments, and guardrail outcomes for post-hoc decision analysis

decision_explainability.methodology: Assessment of trace-based explanation of agent routing and tool decisions

evidence.value: MIT license with full source available; major 2026-04-15 overhaul developed in the open

open_source_code.methodology: Open source assessment of license, source availability, and public development

evidence.value: Tens of thousands of stars, frequent releases, and a large contributor and integration ecosystem
Rapid release cadence continues: Python 0.18.0 released 2026-07-07 (0.17.x through June); repo at ~27,800 stars

community_activity.methodology: Community engagement analysis via GitHub stars, contributor activity, and release cadence

evidence.value: Minimal-primitives design (agents, handoffs, guardrails, sessions); a working agent in a few lines of code

ease_of_integration.methodology: Integration complexity assessment from install to working multi-agent app

evidence.value: Lightweight stateless runner scales horizontally; throughput bounded by model provider rate limits

scalability.methodology: Assessment of stateless runner scaling and provider rate limit constraints

evidence.value: Free MIT-licensed SDK; only costs are model API rates from the chosen provider

cost_predictability.methodology: Pricing model analysis of free framework plus pay-per-token model usage

evidence.value: Built-in tracing dashboard plus OTel and third-party exporters (Logfire, Langfuse, W&B, and more)

monitoring_capabilities.methodology: Monitoring features assessment including built-in and third-party observability

evidence.source: OpenAI: New tools for building agents
evidence.value: Explicitly positioned as production-ready successor to experimental Swarm; matured further with the 2026-04-15 overhaul

production_readiness.methodology: Maturity assessment from release history, API stability, and enterprise adoption

customer-support.notes: Handoffs between triage and specialist agents plus guardrails make this a flagship use case

research-assistant.notes: Hosted web/file search tools and multi-agent orchestration suit research pipelines

code-generation.notes: Capable with code interpreter and function tools, though not specialized for coding workflows

data-analysis.notes: Code interpreter, file search, and structured outputs work well for analysis agents

content-creation.notes: Multi-agent writer/editor pipelines with output guardrails are straightforward to build

best_for: Teams building production multi-agent systems with handoffs and guardrails
Developers wanting built-in tracing and observability without extra setup
Organizations standardizing on the OpenAI/Responses API ecosystem
Projects needing model flexibility across 100+ LLMs via LiteLLM
strengths: Minimal, well-designed primitives: agents, handoffs, guardrails, sessions
Best-in-class built-in tracing with dashboard and OTel/third-party exporters
First-class input/output guardrails with tripwire enforcement
MIT-licensed and fully open source in Python and TypeScript
Provider-agnostic via LiteLLM (100+ models) despite Responses-API-native design
Free framework; costs limited to model usage
limitations: Custom function tools still run unsandboxed (native sandbox added April 2026 covers harness/shell/file work, Python first)
Tracing exports run data to OpenAI by default unless explicitly disabled
Some hosted tools and tracing features work best only with OpenAI models
April 2026 overhaul introduced breaking changes requiring migration (dropped Python 3.9, requires openai v2.x, refusals now raise ModelRefusalError)
Python package still versioned 0.x with frequent releases despite production positioning
Less opinionated about deployment, requiring infrastructure decisions from the team

tool_support: Hosted tools (web search, file search, code interpreter, computer use)
metadata.pricing: Free (MIT); pay only model API rates
